text:
We're very much up and running at the Paris Games, with plenty of Irish interest on Day 2.

The rowers get us going at 9.40am as Aifric Keogh and Fiona Murtagh compete in the women's pair heats. Paul O'Donovan and Fintan McCarthy begin their quest for a medal at 11.20am in Men's Lightweight Double Sculls heats.

In the middle of a busy morning of rowing action Mona McSharry enters the pool for the Women's 100m Breaststroke heats, which start at 10.27am.

At 10.32am Belfast boxer Aidan Walsh is in the ring to face Frenchman Makan Traore in the last 32 of the men's 71kg. Grainne Walsh and Jack Marley fight Anna Luca Hamori and Mateusz Bereznicki at 4.22pm and 7.32pm respectively.

There's a day of equestrian competition as the eventing team of Susie Berry (Wellfields Lincoln), Sarah Ennis (Action Lady M) and Austin O'Connor (Colorado Blue) are up.

The Ireland women's rugby sevens team gets started too with a couple of tough Pool B games against Great Britain and South Africa, while our sailors Robert Dickson and Sean Waddilove are on the water this afternoon.

You can follow the Olympic Games action all day with our live tracker on RTÉ Sport Online and the RTÉ News app, watch live from 9.15am-10.30pm on RTÉ2 and RTÉ Player, and listen to updates on RTÉ Radio 1 and 2fm.

(In order of start time)

1027-1043Women's 100m Breaststroke:Mona McSharry(Heat 5)

2010-2022Women's 100m Breaststroke semi-finals:Mona McSharry(if qualified)

1032Men’s 71kg Rd 32: Makan Traore (Fra) vAidan Walsh (Irl)

1622Women’s 66kg Rd 32:Grainne Walsh (Irl)v Anna Luca Hamori (Hun)

1932Men’s 92kg Rd 16:Jack Marley (Irl)v Mateusz Bereznicki (Pol)

0930-1430Eventing – Team and Individual: Susie Berry (Wellfields Lincoln), Sarah Ennis (Action Lady M), Austin O’Connor (Colorado Blue)

0940Women’s Pair heats: Aifric Keogh, Fiona Murtagh (Heat 2)

1000Men’s Pair heats: Ross Corrigan, Nathan Timoney (Heat 1)

1030Women’s Lightweight Double Sculls heats: Margaret Cremen, Aoife Casey (Heat 1)

1120Men’s Lightweight Double Sculls: Paul O’Donovan, Fintan McCarthy (Heat 3)

1140Women’s Four heats: Emily Hegarty, Natalie Long, Eimear Lambe, Imogen Magner (Heat 2)

Women’s Pool Phase – Pool B:

1430Ireland v Great Britain

1800Ireland v South Africa

Skiff Races 1-3 (1440-1520, 1532-1612, 1624-1704): Robert Dickson, Sean Waddilove

title: Paris 2024: Irish in action on Day 2 of Olympic Games
description: We're very much up and running at the Paris Games, with plenty of Irish interest on Day 2. Here's your full schedule for Sunday's events.
